jokes.text: CHARLIE: we're not selling a game for squares, we're selling a game for, like, everyday people like us, man.
jokes.setup: Dennis suggests they should have dressed up to sell their game
jokes.punchline: Charlie earnestly argues they represent 'everyday people' when they're clearly deranged
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Charlie's complete lack of self-awareness about how abnormal they are is classic Sunny humor

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: we're not selling a game for squares, we're selling a game for, like, everyday people like us, man.

jokes.text: FRANK: I mean, we used to play 'Purple Nurple,' 'Sock Full of Quarters,' 'Kick the Jew'...
jokes.setup: Frank nostalgically recalls childhood games being more violent
jokes.punchline: The escalation from mildly violent to overtly antisemitic 'game'
jokes.explanation: Classic Frank - casually dropping horrific content as if it's normal. The escalation structure works well

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: I mean, we used to play 'Purple Nurple,' 'Sock Full of Quarters,' 'Kick the Jew'...

jokes.text: FRANK: 'Franken MacChardeeden.'
jokes.setup: Frank suggests changing the game name to include his input
jokes.punchline: The completely nonsensical portmanteau that makes no linguistic sense
jokes.explanation: Frank's complete inability to contribute anything sensible, delivered with total confidence

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: 'Franken MacChardeeden.'

jokes.text: MAC: First of all, your name isn't 'Franken.'
jokes.setup: Frank's ridiculous name suggestion
jokes.punchline: Mac focusing on the least problematic aspect of the terrible suggestion
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man response that highlights the absurdity by understating it

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: First of all, your name isn't 'Franken.'

jokes.text: CHARLIE: And I will not yield to the gentleman. DEE: 'Cause you have no honor, you goddamn bitch. You have no honor!
jokes.setup: Mac explains they're in formal arbitration about the game name
jokes.punchline: Charlie using formal parliamentary language immediately followed by Dee's profane outburst
jokes.explanation: Excellent contrast between formal and crude language, perfect character dynamics

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: And I will not yield to the gentleman.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: 'Cause you have no honor, you goddamn bitch. You have no honor!

jokes.text: FRANK: Then let's call it 'Chardee MacDennis 2: Electric Boogaloo.'
jokes.setup: The gang arguing about the game name and rules
jokes.punchline: Frank's completely random reference to the breakdancing movie sequel
jokes.explanation: Random pop culture reference that becomes the episode title - Frank's non-sequitur logic

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: Then let's call it 'Chardee MacDennis 2: Electric Boogaloo.'

jokes.text: DENNIS: What does that have to do with anything? What made you just think of that? It's not a break-dancing competition. There's no electricity involved.
jokes.setup: Frank's random 'Electric Boogaloo' suggestion
jokes.punchline: Dennis's logical breakdown of why the reference makes no sense whatsoever
jokes.explanation: Dennis as the rational voice pointing out Frank's complete randomness

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: What does that have to do with anything? What made you just think of that? It's not a break-dancing competition. There's no electricity involved.

jokes.text: MAN: Fight milk. (crow caws) Crow's egg.
jokes.setup: Andy watching their promotional video
jokes.punchline: Completely unrelated product placement in their game commercial
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random product placement that makes no sense in a game commercial

dialogue.line: Fight milk.

jokes.text: But more better. And different enough legally. I'm a lawyer. Shh! I'm a lawyer in this part.
jokes.setup: Video explaining how their game relates to other board games
jokes.punchline: Charlie's nonsensical grammar and his stage whisper about being a lawyer 'in this part'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie moment - illiteracy ('more better') combined with his delusional belief he can play a lawyer

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: But more better. And different enough legally. I'm a lawyer. Shh! I'm a lawyer in this part.

jokes.text: Regular board games should be called... 'I'm bored... games.' I don't get that one.
jokes.setup: Video making a pun about board games
jokes.punchline: Immediate admission that the pun doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about a bad pun being bad, self-aware comedy failure

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Regular board games should be called... 'I'm bored... games.'

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: I don't get that one.

jokes.text: We always win. And they always lose.
jokes.setup: Promotional video showing the game's competitive nature
jokes.punchline: The smug declaration followed by Charlie asking what they're doing (implying they're clearly losing)
jokes.explanation: Immediate contradiction between their boast and reality

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: We always win.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: And they always lose.

jokes.text: CHARLIE: What are you guys doing over there? Oh, shit. (static hissing)
jokes.setup: Professional promotional video
jokes.punchline: Charlie breaking character and cursing, followed by technical difficulties
jokes.explanation: Amateur video production disasters, Charlie's inability to stay professional

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: What are you guys doing over there? Oh, shit.

jokes.text: W-Why'd you put that in? Yeah, what was that? Factually accurate.
jokes.setup: Questioning why the cursing/technical problems were left in the video
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact defense that it's 'factually accurate'
jokes.explanation: Mac's complete misunderstanding of how promotional videos should work

dialogue.line: W-Why'd you put that in?
Yeah, what was that?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Factually accurate.

jokes.text: Everyone trying to convince themselves they can play without caring about winning, followed by synchronized exhaling
jokes.setup: They need to play casually for the business pitch
jokes.punchline: Their obvious inability to be casual, culminating in ridiculous group breathing exercises
jokes.explanation: Great physical comedy showing how they're trying to convince themselves they can be normal. The synchronized exhaling is a perfect visual gag.

dialogue.line: Everyone trying to convince themselves they can play without caring about winning, followed by synchronized exhaling

jokes.text: DENNIS: We'll keep our emotions in check. MAC: Yeah. CHARLIE: Sure. DENNIS: Just be like: (exhales) (all exhaling) CHARLIE: Exactly. (exhaling continues)
jokes.setup: Dennis suggests they'll keep emotions in check
jokes.punchline: The synchronized breathing exercise that becomes increasingly ridiculous
jokes.explanation: Mild physical comedy with the group breathing exercise, showing their misguided attempt at zen

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: We'll keep our emotions in check.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: Sure.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Just be like: (exhales) (all exhaling)

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: Exactly. (exhaling continues)

jokes.text: DENNIS: Gentlemen... suck my dick.
jokes.setup: Formal, sportsmanlike opening ceremony with polite 'good luck' exchanges
jokes.punchline: Dennis immediately breaking the fake civility with crude hostility
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds up formal sportsmanship then immediately demolishes it

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Gentlemen... suck my dick.

jokes.text: Yeah, we used to do the Maori war dance, but that-that got us a little too riled up.
jokes.setup: Explaining their intimidation ceremony
jokes.punchline: Casual admission they had to dial back from even more inappropriate cultural appropriation
jokes.explanation: Their complete lack of cultural sensitivity presented as practical concern

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Yeah, we used to do the Maori war dance, but that-that got us a little too riled up.

jokes.text: Well, 'cause we're not cats. We're not cats. So, then, how could we...
jokes.setup: Explaining why they changed from Thundercats to Thundermen
jokes.punchline: Their literal interpretation of the cartoon name as requiring actual feline status
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mac and Charlie logic - completely missing the point of metaphorical team names

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Well, 'cause we're not cats. We're not cats. So, then, how could we...

jokes.text: FRANK: It's four Fs. I didn't know it was gonna come off like that. DEE: Pretty sure you did.
jokes.setup: Frank's flag being described as 'unfortunate'
jokes.punchline: Frank's disingenuous claim about not knowing four F's would look like a swastika
jokes.explanation: Frank's pattern of 'accidentally' creating offensive symbols, Dee's deadpan calling him out

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: It's four Fs. I didn't know it was gonna come off like that.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: Pretty sure you did.

jokes.text: What Philly celebrity would you most want to have a drink with? DEE: Bill Cosby. DENNIS: Yes! All right. Yeah! Point for us.
jokes.setup: Trivia question about Philadelphia celebrities
jokes.punchline: Dee choosing Bill Cosby (post-scandal) and Dennis enthusiastically agreeing
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about their terrible judgment and outdated questions

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: Bill Cosby.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Yes! All right. Yeah! Point for us.

jokes.text: FRANK: Uh, penis. What? Uh, lip-penis. What? No, look, look. Oh, hard penis. A hard clay penis.
jokes.setup: Clay sculpting game where partners guess what you're making
jokes.punchline: Frank's increasingly desperate penis-related guesses
jokes.explanation: Crude escalation humor, somewhat predictable for Frank's character

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: Uh, penis. What? Uh, lip-penis. What? No, look, look. Oh, hard penis. A hard clay penis.

jokes.text: DEE: Dee, I'm gonna put your head in a box if you don't start getting the answer right.
jokes.setup: Frustration with Dee's wrong guesses in the clay game
jokes.punchline: Casual threat of decapitation over a board game
jokes.explanation: Their casual escalation to extreme violence over trivial game disputes

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: Dee, I'm gonna put your head in a box if you don't start getting the answer right.

jokes.text: This is not a woman's head in a box, you sick freak. This is a woman's head in a freezer. And it's supposed to represent the preservation of love forever and ever.
jokes.setup: Dennis criticizing Charlie's clay sculpture
jokes.punchline: Charlie's elaborate justification for his disturbing sculpture as romantic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie - taking something horrifying and presenting it as romantic with complete sincerity

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: This is not a woman's head in a box, you sick freak.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: This is a woman's head in a freezer. And it's supposed to represent the preservation of love forever and ever.

jokes.text: The bottle reconstruction puzzle where they have to drink the broken bottle and Charlie swallows glass
jokes.setup: Puzzle challenge to put a bottle back together
jokes.punchline: They literally reconstruct it by drinking it, with Charlie casually mentioning swallowing glass
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - they solve the 'puzzle' in the most literal and dangerous way possible. Charlie's casual mention of swallowing glass is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: It held! I swallowed a little glass, but it held, man!
jokes.setup: Puzzle challenge to put a broken bottle back together
jokes.punchline: Charlie celebrating success despite apparently eating glass in the process
jokes.explanation: Charlie's priorities - celebrating the game victory while casually mentioning self-harm

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: It held! I swallowed a little glass, but it held, man!

jokes.text: we're mainlining wine. It's not beer. We switched Level 2 to wine because you can't mainline beer because it's got bubbles in it and shit. We tried-- it was bad.
jokes.setup: Andy being concerned about IV alcohol administration
jokes.punchline: Mac's matter-of-fact explanation of their IV alcohol experimentation
jokes.explanation: Casual discussion of dangerous medical experimentation as game improvement

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: we're mainlining wine. It's not beer. We switched Level 2 to wine because you can't mainline beer because it's got bubbles in it and shit. We tried-- it was bad.

jokes.text: (bad Mexican accent): Okay, homie. Uh... Orale, homes. Yeah, I can do that. Yeah. Okay, man. Nachos.
jokes.setup: Accent challenge where Dennis lands on Brazil/Latin America
jokes.punchline: Dennis's incredibly racist stereotypical 'Mexican' accent with random Spanish words
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Dennis's casual racism, though somewhat predictable

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: (bad Mexican accent): Okay, homie. Uh... Orale, homes. Yeah, I can do that. Yeah. Okay, man. Nachos.

jokes.text: Oh, real racist with it, huh? Yeah, that is extremely racist-- and by the way, that's more of a Los Angeles sort of Mexican cholo vibe. Let's go... let's go see the Dodgers play the Lakers.
jokes.setup: Dennis's racist accent
jokes.punchline: Mac and Charlie calling out the racism while adding even more racist stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Self-aware racism critique that immediately becomes more racism - classic Sunny

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Oh, real racist with it, huh? Yeah, that is extremely racist-- and by the way, that's more of a Los Angeles sort of Mexican cholo vibe.

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: Let's go... let's go see the Dodgers play the Lakers.

jokes.text: (Philadelphia accent): Hallelujah. Looks like the tide's turned. Yeah, looks like we got an advantage over yous guys, huh, Den?
jokes.setup: Dee spinning Philadelphia for the accent challenge
jokes.punchline: Dee's terrible attempt at her own city's accent despite living there
jokes.explanation: Dee's complete inability to do her own regional accent she's heard her whole life

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: (Philadelphia accent): Hallelujah. Looks like the tide's turned. Yeah, looks like we got an advantage over yous guys, huh, Den?

jokes.text: Physical challenge: 'Hands on a bird body!' with everyone having to keep their hands on Dee
jokes.setup: Physical endurance challenge introduction
jokes.punchline: The 'bird body' they have to touch is Dee herself (they call her a bird)
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to the running bird joke, made into a physical challenge. The uncomfortable nature of the challenge adds to the comedy.

dialogue.line: Physical challenge: 'Hands on a bird body!' with everyone having to keep their hands on Dee

jokes.text: Vhy are you zo uncomfortable? Because the closeness of your hand to her breast... which is making you want to have sex with her?
jokes.setup: Andy asking Dennis why he's uncomfortable
jokes.punchline: Andy's hilariously wrong interpretation that Dennis is attracted to Dee
jokes.explanation: Complete misunderstanding of the sibling dynamic and source of Dennis's discomfort

dialogue.speaker: ANDY
dialogue.line: Vhy are you zo uncomfortable? Because the closeness of your hand to her breast... which is making you want to have sex with her?

jokes.text: FRANK: I can't do it, 'mang.' I can't do it. DENNIS: You barely even put your hand on there already. MAC: Hang in there, man. FRANK: I know, but... Ah! I can't do it, I can't do it! Oh! Ha! I can't even do it. Ay, ay, ay!
jokes.setup: Frank struggling with the hands-on-Dee challenge
jokes.punchline: His immediate failure and dramatic Spanish exclamations while maintaining the accent
jokes.explanation: Standard Frank physical comedy with his dramatic overreaction to touching Dee

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: I can't do it, 'mang.' I can't do it.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: You barely even put your hand on there already.

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: Hang in there, man.

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: I know, but... Ah! I can't do it, I can't do it! Oh! Ha! I can't even do it. Ay, ay, ay!

jokes.text: Shits and Ladders. Sort of a play on Chutes and Ladders.
jokes.setup: Drawing a Level 3 challenge card
jokes.punchline: Crude pun on classic board game involving literal defecation
jokes.explanation: Simple scatological pun, low-hanging fruit but fits the show's crude humor

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Shits and Ladders. Sort of a play on Chutes and Ladders.

jokes.text: Each member must take a laxative and get on a ladder. The one who can hold it in the longest wins. Also known as Shoots and Splatters. Poops and Bladders!
jokes.setup: Explaining the 'Shits and Ladders' game
jokes.punchline: Multiple crude variations on board game names
jokes.explanation: Escalating scatological puns, juvenile but consistent with their mentality

dialogue.line: Each member must take a laxative and get on a ladder. The one who can hold it in the longest wins. Also known as Shoots and Splatters. Poops and Bladders!

jokes.text: That isn't a laxative, is it, Frank? Nope. Welcome to Level 4, (echoing): Horror.
jokes.setup: Charlie feeling strange effects from what he thought was a laxative
jokes.punchline: Frank revealing he drugged them and introducing the secret horror level
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and callback - Frank's 'crazy' ideas were actually his plan all along

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: That isn't a laxative, is it, Frank?

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: Nope. Welcome to Level 4, (echoing): Horror.

jokes.text: Everything has to be based on an existing board game! That's the whole thing! Oh, but it is, Dennis. Ever hear of a game called... Operation?
jokes.setup: Dennis protesting that Frank's torture scenario isn't based on a board game
jokes.punchline: Frank's twisted logic connecting his torture to the Operation board game
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy - Frank's twisted but technically correct logic justifying torture as a board game

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Everything has to be based on an existing board game! That's the whole thing!

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: Oh, but it is, Dennis. Ever hear of a game called... Operation?

jokes.text: CHARLIE: You guys quit! You quit and we won the game! Finally. Finally, I won the game. ANDY: This is not what I signed up for, you donkey-brained maniac!
jokes.setup: Charlie celebrating his first game victory
jokes.punchline: Andy's outburst calling Charlie 'donkey-brained' - a very specific Charlie insult
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for Charlie finally winning, with the suspicious use of his signature insult

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: You guys quit! You quit and we won the game! Finally. Finally, I won the game.

dialogue.speaker: ANDY
dialogue.line: This is not what I signed up for, you donkey-brained maniac!

jokes.text: This game is totally insane! Don't give up on the game! The game is fine! No, no, no, no. No, this isn't representative of us...
jokes.setup: Andy protesting the torture scenario
jokes.punchline: Dennis still trying to salvage the business deal while being electrocuted
jokes.explanation: Dennis's desperation to maintain professional facade during obvious disaster

dialogue.speaker: ANDY
dialogue.line: This game is totally insane!

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Don't give up on the game! The game is fine!

dialogue.speaker: ANDY
dialogue.line: No, no, no, no. No, this isn't representative of us...

jokes.text: You guys quit! You quit and we won the game! Finally. Finally, I won the game.
jokes.setup: Everyone quitting Frank's torture game
jokes.punchline: Charlie celebrating his first victory despite being covered in blood and having defecated himself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie priorities - winning the game matters more than his health and dignity

dialogue.speaker: CHARLIE
dialogue.line: You guys quit! You quit and we won the game! Finally. Finally, I won the game.

jokes.text: This is not what I signed up for, you donkey-brained maniac!
jokes.setup: Andy's outrage at Frank's torture game
jokes.punchline: Andy using the gang's specific insult 'donkey-brained'
jokes.explanation: Callback to series running gag, hints at Andy's true connection to the group

dialogue.speaker: ANDY
dialogue.line: This is not what I signed up for, you donkey-brained maniac!

jokes.text: 'Mattel' is spelled wrong on his business card. That says 'Matel.'
jokes.setup: Discovering Andy's true identity
jokes.punchline: The obvious fake business card with a simple misspelling
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag revealing the amateur nature of Frank's deception

dialogue.line: 'Mattel' is spelled wrong on his business card. That says 'Matel.'

jokes.text: FRANK (recorded): Welcome to the home of Wolf Cola...
jokes.setup: Calling the number on Andy's business card
jokes.punchline: It's Frank's own answering machine for his other fake business
jokes.explanation: Great callback revealing Frank's elaborate deception and connecting to series mythology

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Welcome to the home of Wolf Cola...

jokes.text: DENNIS: Oh, okay, Frank, so you clearly just hired this guy so that you could win and put your stamp on the game! FRANK: No, there's no truth in that at all. No. Tell 'em, Andy. (door slams)
jokes.setup: Dennis confronting Frank about the scam
jokes.punchline: Andy immediately fleeing when asked to back up Frank's lie
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy beat with Andy's immediate escape undermining Frank's denial

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Oh, okay, Frank, so you clearly just hired this guy so that you could win and put your stamp on the game!

dialogue.speaker: FRANK
dialogue.line: No, there's no truth in that at all. No. Tell 'em, Andy.

jokes.text: DENNIS: So, then, if this isn't about selling the game anymore... Well, then it's about winning.
jokes.setup: The group realizing the whole pitch was fake
jokes.punchline: Dennis's immediate pivot back to their competitive obsession
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis moment - his ability to immediately refocus on what really matters to him: winning

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: So, then, if this isn't about selling the game anymore... Well, then it's about winning.

jokes.text: THE WAITRESS: What? Oh, my God, what is that smell? Is that poop? Did you poop in your pants, Charlie?
jokes.setup: The Waitress arriving for Charlie's public humiliation challenge
jokes.punchline: Immediately noticing Charlie has defecated himself
jokes.explanation: Escalating Charlie's humiliation, sets up the challenge's difficulty

dialogue.speaker: THE WAITRESS
dialogue.line: What? Oh, my God, what is that smell? Is that poop? Did you poop in your pants, Charlie?

jokes.text: Charlie Kelly, I have been waiting for this for a long time, you poop-smelling (distorted): trash person. Your face, it looks like a troll! You look like you have troll features!
jokes.setup: The Waitress beginning her berating of Charlie
jokes.punchline: Her long-suppressed rage erupting with oddly specific insults about his troll-like appearance
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - years of suppressed frustration with Charlie exploding out

dialogue.speaker: THE WAITRESS
dialogue.line: Charlie Kelly, I have been waiting for this for a long time, you poop-smelling (distorted): trash person. Your face, it looks like a troll! You look like you have troll features!

jokes.text: No, no, you cried like a baby. Yeah, pretty much right when the waitress started talking, you cried like a little bitch.
jokes.setup: Charlie believing he won by not crying
jokes.punchline: The gang revealing he immediately broke down crying
jokes.explanation: Classic deflation of Charlie's moment of triumph with harsh reality

dialogue.speaker: MAC
dialogue.line: No, no, you cried like a baby.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Yeah, pretty much right when the waitress started talking, you cried like a little bitch.

jokes.text: You-you must have been hallucinating from all the blood poisoning.
jokes.setup: Charlie's false memory of winning
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that Charlie was literally dying from blood poisoning
jokes.explanation: Casual medical emergency treated as minor detail in their game obsession

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: You-you must have been hallucinating from all the blood poisoning.

jokes.text: But now that you're conscious, you know... Dee, are you, uh, all set back there? Yep.
jokes.setup: Dennis's fake compassion about not destroying Charlie's pieces
jokes.punchline: Revealing they were waiting for him to wake up so he could watch them do it
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - builds false hope then reveals even crueler reality. Masterful structure.

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: But now that you're conscious, you know... Dee, are you, uh, all set back there?

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: Yep.

jokes.text: Loser. Yeah! (both taunting, screeching) Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah! (both honking)
jokes.setup: Destroying Charlie's game pieces
jokes.punchline: Their completely unhinged celebration with inhuman screeching and honking
jokes.explanation: Final escalation showing their complete lack of humanity, perfect ending to their cruelty

dialogue.speaker: DENNIS
dialogue.line: Loser.

dialogue.speaker: DEE
dialogue.line: Yeah!
Yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah, yeah!
